item.title: 🇬🇧🇺🇦 King Charles urges unyielding resolve for Ukraine in historic Congress speech
item.description: The British monarch addressed the US Congress emphasizing continued support for Ukraine and NATO unity, using symbolic soft power to reinforce transatlantic commitment during a critical period for Western alliances.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ Hezbollah drone kills Israeli contractor in southern Lebanon
item.description: An Israeli civilian contractor was killed and his son wounded by an FPV drone strike while operating an excavator in southern Lebanon, where IDF forces are demolishing Hezbollah infrastructure.

item.title: 🇪🇺🤝 US and NATO face frustrating marriage crisis, Belgian defense minister says
item.description: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken described strained transatlantic relations as a marriage crisis, urging NATO unity while acknowledging growing tensions in the alliance during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🏛️ Ex-FBI Director Comey indicted again over seashell photo seen as Trump threat
item.description: James Comey faces a second indictment over a social media post showing seashells arranged in a pattern that officials interpreted as a potential threat against President Trump.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US ties UN dues payment to China curbs, report reveals
item.description: The United States, owing billions in unpaid fees to the UN, has reportedly attached conditions to its dues payment including measures targeting China, as the organization faces imminent financial collapse.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE announces exit from OPEC and OPEC+ amid Iran war energy crisis
item.description: The United Arab Emirates will leave OPEC on May 1, dealing a major blow to the oil cartel as the Iran-US conflict strains Gulf relations and reshapes global energy dynamics. The exit weakens OPEC+ influence and raises prospects of a price war.

item.title: 🇸🇩💔 Sexual violence in Sudan creates mental health crisis, UN warns
item.description: The WHO reports a mental health emergency in Sudan as sexual violence affects tens of thousands of women and girls amid the ongoing civil war. Access to healthcare is severely limited, with survivors facing stigma and lack of trained medical staff.

item.title: ⚖️🇺🇸 Jury deliberates in trial of alleged ISIS militant charged in Kabul airport bombing
item.description: Federal jurors in Virginia are weighing charges against Mohammad Sharifullah, accused of helping plan the 2021 Abbey Gate suicide bombing that killed 13 US service members and 160 Afghans during the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan.

item.title: 🇨🇦💰 Canada reports lower deficit as oil price surge boosts revenues
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled a spring budget showing a deficit 14% lower than projected, aided by rising oil prices. The government announced a new sovereign wealth fund for infrastructure and domestic investments.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices surge as US moves to extend Iran port blockade
item.description: Oil prices extended their rally on reports the US will extend its blockade of Iranian ports, likely prolonging supply disruptions from the Middle East. The move signals no immediate end to the conflict that has constrained global oil flows.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump says Iran in collapse, wants US to reopen Hormuz Strait
item.description: President Trump stated that Iran has said it is in a state of collapse and wants the US to open the Strait of Hormuz while sorting out its leadership. The comment suggests Tehran is seeking an off-ramp from the prolonged conflict.

item.title: 💸 US imposes sanctions on 35 entities for Iran sanctions evasion
item.description: The US government ratcheted up pressure on Tehran, sanctioning 35 entities and individuals for roles in Iran shadow banking sector, and threatened sanctions against banks dealing with Chinese teapot refineries paying Hormuz tolls.

item.title: 📉 US bond markets split as Iran war tests Federal Reserve outlook
item.description: The Middle East conflict has splintered the US fixed-income market, with corporate credit investors behaving as if the Iran war is over while Treasury holders remain focused on inflation risks that could limit Fed easing.

item.title: 🚢 First Japan-linked tanker crosses Hormuz since Iran war began
item.description: A Panama-flagged tanker carrying 2 million barrels of Saudi oil crossed the Strait of Hormuz, becoming the first Japan-linked crude tanker to do so since the conflict began, a sign of tentative normalization.

item.title: 🇨🇳✈️ Chinese Scientists Pioneer Converting Carbon Dioxide Into Jet Fuel
item.description: A Chinese research team has developed a method to synthesize jet fuel from carbon dioxide as soaring oil prices from the Iran conflict drive interest in alternative aviation fuels.

item.title: 🇯🇵🕌 Japan Faces Tensions Over Growing Muslim Communities and Mosque Construction
item.description: Resistance to new mosques in Japan is exposing social tensions as the Muslim population grows, highlighting challenges of integration in a traditionally homogeneous society.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇯🇵⚔️ China Condemns Japan Over Neo-Militarism Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson calls for countries to resist Japan's reckless moves toward neo-militarism as Tokyo expands defense capabilities.

item.title: ⚖️ Former FBI Director James Comey Indicted Over Seashell Photo
item.description: James Comey faces criminal charges for a second time after posting an Instagram photo of seashells arranged as "86 47," which officials interpreted as a threat against President Trump. The indictment marks an unprecedented escalation in tensions between the former FBI director and the Trump administration.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles Addresses Congress in Rare Speech
item.description: King Charles III became only the second British monarch to address the U.S. Congress, urging American lawmakers to maintain global leadership and reject isolationism. The speech came amid heightened geopolitical tensions between the United States and Iran.

item.title: 🏛️ Powell Prepares for Final Fed Meeting as Chair
item.description: Jerome Powell is set to chair what is likely his final Federal Reserve policy meeting, with debate swirling about his legacy after eight years leading the central bank. His tenure has been marked by standing up to political pressure while navigating economic crises.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇯🇵 SK Group Chairman Urges South Korea-Japan Economic Integration
item.description: SK Group Chairman Choi Tae-won called for South Korea to pursue economic integration with Japan and lead a broader Asian economic alliance to compete in the AI era. The remarks come as U.S.-China rivalry intensifies in technology development.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea ICBMs Can Strike US Mainland, Pentagon Official Warns
item.description: A senior US defense official confirmed North Korea has the capability to strike the US mainland with intercontinental ballistic missiles, underscoring the growing ballistic missile threat amid Pyongyang's diplomatic outreach to Trump.

item.title: 💰 Global Military Spending Hits Record $4 Trillion
item.description: Global defense expenditure reached a historic high of $4 trillion, with the United States, China, and Russia accounting for more than half of total spending, according to new research reflecting intensified great power competition.

item.title: 🇪🇺⚔️ NATO weaknesses exposed by Iran war, analysis finds
item.description: A Politico EU analysis identifies five key ways the Iran conflict reveals NATO is unprepared to fight Russia, from munitions depletion to alliance coordination gaps.

item.title: 🛢️🚢 Hormuz shipping traffic remains muted as US-Iran talks stall
item.description: Only 7 ships crossed the Strait in 24 hours, a fraction of the 140 daily passages before the war. Data shows Iran has moved oil through the US blockade while negotiations remain deadlocked.
